qa_pairs.question: When was Microsoft founded and what does it develop?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft was founded in 1975. The company develops and supports software, services, devices, and solutions that deliver new value for customers and help people and businesses realize their full potential.
qa_pairs.evidence: Founded in 1975, we develop and support software, services, devices, and solutions that deliver new value for customers and help people and businesses realize their full potential.

qa_pairs.question: What is Copilot for Microsoft 365?
qa_pairs.answer: Copilot for Microsoft 365 combines AI with business data in the Microsoft Graph and Microsoft 365 applications. It provides AI-powered assistance integrated into the productivity suite that organizations already use.
qa_pairs.evidence: Copilot for Microsoft 365 combines AI with business data in the Microsoft Graph and Microsoft 365 applications.

qa_pairs.question: What custom AI hardware has Microsoft introduced for cloud infrastructure?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft has introduced purpose-built cloud infrastructure for AI workloads including a custom AI accelerator called Azure Maia and a custom in-house central processing unit called Azure Cobalt. These complement the company's supercomputing power for AI at scale.
qa_pairs.evidence: We have introduced purpose-built cloud infrastructure for AI workloads including a custom AI accelerator, Azure Maia, and a custom in-house central processing unit, Azure Cobalt.

qa_pairs.question: What is Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI and what role does Azure play?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft has a long-term partnership with OpenAI, a leading AI research and deployment company. Microsoft deploys OpenAI's models across its consumer and enterprise products. As OpenAI's exclusive cloud provider, Azure powers all of OpenAI's workloads, and Microsoft has increased investments in specialized supercomputing systems to accelerate OpenAI's research.
qa_pairs.evidence: We have a long-term partnership with OpenAI, a leading AI research and deployment company. We deploy OpenAI’s models across our consumer and enterprise products. As OpenAI’s exclusive cloud provider, Azure powers all of OpenAI's workloads. We have also increased our investments in the development and deployment of specialized supercomputing systems to accelerate OpenAI’s research.

qa_pairs.question: What is GitHub Copilot and what does it do for developers?
qa_pairs.answer: GitHub Copilot is at the forefront of AI-powered software development, giving developers a tool to write code easier and faster. It is part of Microsoft's developer tool chain available for everyone, regardless of technical experience, across all platforms.
qa_pairs.evidence: GitHub Copilot is at the forefront of AI-powered software development, giving developers a tool to write code easier and faster. From GitHub to Visual Studio, we provide a developer tool chain for everyone, no matter the technical experience, across all platforms.

qa_pairs.question: What is Microsoft Fabric?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end, unified analytics platform that brings together all the data and analytics tools that organizations need. It is part of the Microsoft Intelligent Data Platform, which fully integrates databases, analytics, and governance.
qa_pairs.evidence: The Microsoft Intelligent Data Platform fully integrates databases, analytics, and governance. Microsoft Fabric is an end-to-end, unified analytics platform that brings together all the data and analytics tools that organizations need.

qa_pairs.question: What sustainability commitments did Microsoft make in 2020?
qa_pairs.answer: In 2020, Microsoft committed to being a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030. Since then, the company has seen major changes in the technology sector and its understanding of what it will take to meet these climate goals.
qa_pairs.evidence: In 2020, we committed to being a carbon negative, water positive, and zero waste company by 2030.

qa_pairs.question: In which sustainability areas is Microsoft on track and in which areas is it not yet on track?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft is on track in four areas: reducing direct operational emissions (Scope 1 and 2), accelerating carbon removal, designing for circularity to minimize waste and reusing cloud hardware, and improving biodiversity. It is not yet on track in two areas: reducing indirect emissions (Scope 3) and reducing water use in datacenter operations.
qa_pairs.evidence:
Reducing our direct operational emissions (Scope 1 and 2).
•
Accelerating carbon removal.
•
Designing for circularity to minimize waste and reusing cloud hardware.
•
Improving biodiversity and protecting more land than we use.
At the same time, there are two areas where we’re not yet on track, and in each of these we are intensively engaged in work to identify and pursue additional breakthroughs. These are:
•
Reducing our indirect emissions (Scope 3).
•
Reducing our water use and replenishing more water than we consume in our datacenter operations.

qa_pairs.question: What are Microsoft's three reportable operating segments?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft operates its business and reports its financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. These segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of key businesses and enable alignment of strategies across the organization.
qa_pairs.evidence: We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses.

qa_pairs.question: What is Azure and how does it generate revenue?
qa_pairs.answer: Azure is a comprehensive set of cloud services that offer developers, IT professionals, and enterprises freedom to build, deploy, and manage applications on any platform or device. Azure revenue is mainly affected by infrastructure-as-a-service and platform-as-a-service consumption-based services, and per user-based services such as Enterprise Mobility + Security.
qa_pairs.evidence: Azure is a comprehensive set of cloud services that offer developers, IT professionals, and enterprises freedom to build, deploy, and manage applications on any platform or device. Customers can use Azure through our global network of datacenters for computing, networking, storage, mobile and web application services, AI, Internet of Things (“IoT”), cognitive services, and machine learning. Azure enables customers to devote more resources to development and use of applications that benefit their organizations, rather than managing on-premises hardware and software. Azure revenue is mainly affected by infrastructure-as-a-service and platform-as-a-service consumption-based services, and per user-based services such as Enterprise Mobility + Security.

qa_pairs.question: Who are the main competitors to Azure?
qa_pairs.answer: Azure faces diverse competition from companies such as Amazon, Broadcom, Google, IBM, Oracle, and open source offerings. Microsoft's AI offerings also compete with products from hyperscalers like Amazon and Google, as well as emerging competitors including Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and other open source offerings.
qa_pairs.evidence: Azure faces diverse competition from companies such as Amazon, Broadcom, Google, IBM, Oracle, and open source offerings. Azure’s competitive advantage includes enabling a hybrid cloud, allowing deployment of existing datacenters with our public cloud into a single, cohesive infrastructure, and the ability to run at a scale that meets the needs of businesses of all sizes and complexities. Our AI offerings compete with AI products from hyperscalers such as Amazon and Google, as well as products from other emerging competitors, including Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, and other open source offerings, many of which are also current or potential partners. Our Azure Security offerings include our cloud security solution and security information and event management solution, which compete with companies such as Palo Alto Networks and Cisco. Our Enterprise Mobility + Security offerings also compete with products from a range of competitors including identity vendors, security solution vendors, and numerous other security point solution vendors. We believe our cloud’s global scale, coupled with our broad portfolio of identity and security solutions, allows us to effectively solve complex cybersecurity challenges for our customers and differentiates us from the competition.

qa_pairs.question: What does the More Personal Computing segment primarily comprise?
qa_pairs.answer: The More Personal Computing segment primarily comprises Windows (including OEM licensing and commercial offerings), Devices (including Surface, HoloLens, and PC accessories), Gaming (including Xbox hardware and content/services such as Activision Blizzard), and Search and news advertising (including Bing, Copilot, Microsoft News, and Microsoft Edge).
qa_pairs.evidence:
Our More Personal Computing segment consists of products and services that put customers at the center of the experience with our technology. This segment primarily comprises:
•
Windows, including Windows OEM licensing and other non-volume licensing of the Windows operating system; Windows Commercial, comprising volume licensing of the Windows operating system, Windows cloud services, and other Windows commercial offerings; patent licensing; and Windows Internet of Things.
•
Devices, including Surface, HoloLens, and PC accessories.
•
Gaming, including Xbox hardware and Xbox content and services, comprising first-party content (such as Activision Blizzard) and third-party content, including games and in-game content; Xbox Game Pass and other subscriptions; Xbox Cloud Gaming; advertising; third-party disc royalties; and other cloud services.
•
Search and news advertising, comprising Bing (including Copilot), Microsoft News, Microsoft Edge, and third-party affiliates.

qa_pairs.question: What are Copilot+ PCs?
qa_pairs.answer: Copilot+ PCs are a new class of Windows 11 PCs that are powered by a neural processing unit. These PCs use on-device AI for enhanced performance and features, representing a new category of computing devices focused on AI capabilities.
qa_pairs.evidence: Copilot+ PCs are a new class of Windows 11 PCs that are powered by a neural processing unit. These PCs use on-device AI for enhanced performance and features.

qa_pairs.question: What is Xbox Game Pass?
qa_pairs.answer: Xbox Game Pass is a subscription service and gaming community with access to a curated library of over 400 first- and third-party console and PC titles. It represents the cornerstone of Microsoft's unique gaming experiences and is central to its gaming platform strategy.
qa_pairs.evidence: These unique gaming experiences are the cornerstone of Xbox Game Pass, a subscription service and gaming community with access to a curated library of over 400 first- and third-party console and PC titles.

qa_pairs.question: When did Microsoft complete its acquisition of Activision Blizzard and what was the purchase price?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft completed its acquisition of Activision Blizzard on October 13, 2023, for a total purchase price of $75.4 billion, consisting primarily of cash. The financial results of Activision Blizzard have been included in Microsoft's consolidated financial statements since the acquisition date, and Activision Blizzard is reported as part of the More Personal Computing segment.
qa_pairs.evidence: On October 13, 2023, we completed our acquisition of Activision Blizzard for a total purchase price of $75.4 billion, consisting primarily of cash. The financial results of Activision Blizzard have been included in our consolidated financial statements since the date of the acquisition. Activision Blizzard is reported as part of our More Personal Computing segment.

qa_pairs.question: How many patents does Microsoft hold?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft currently has a portfolio of over 63,000 U.S. and international patents issued and over 23,000 pending worldwide. The company is described as a leader among technology companies in pursuing patents.
qa_pairs.evidence: We are a leader among technology companies in pursuing patents and currently have a portfolio of over 63,000 U.S. and international patents issued and over 23,000 pending worldwide.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two broad categories of OEMs through which Microsoft distributes products?
qa_pairs.answer: The two broad categories are direct OEMs and system builders. Direct OEMs have a relationship managed through a direct agreement between Microsoft and the OEM, including multinational OEMs like Dell, Hewlett-Packard, and Lenovo. System builders are lower-volume PC manufacturers that source Microsoft software primarily through the distributor channel rather than a direct relationship with Microsoft.
qa_pairs.evidence: There are two broad categories of OEMs. The largest category of OEMs are direct OEMs as our relationship with them is managed through a direct agreement between Microsoft and the OEM. We have distribution agreements covering one or more of our products with virtually all the multinational OEMs, including Dell, Hewlett-Packard, Lenovo, and with many regional and local OEMs. The second broad category of OEMs are system builders consisting of lower-volume PC manufacturers, which source Microsoft software for pre-installation and local redistribution primarily through the Microsoft distributor channel rather than through a direct agreement or relationship with Microsoft.

qa_pairs.question: What is Software Assurance (SA)?
qa_pairs.answer: Software Assurance conveys rights to new software and upgrades for perpetual licenses released over the contract period. It also provides support, tools, training, and other licensing benefits to help customers deploy and use software efficiently. SA is required to be purchased with certain volume licensing agreements and is optional with others.
qa_pairs.evidence: Software Assurance (“SA”) conveys rights to new software and upgrades for perpetual licenses released over the contract period. It also provides support, tools, training, and other licensing benefits to help customers deploy and use software efficiently. SA is required to be purchased with certain volume licensing agreements and is an optional purchase with others.

qa_pairs.question: What is an Enterprise Agreement and who is it designed for?
qa_pairs.answer: Enterprise Agreements offer large organizations a manageable volume licensing program with flexibility to buy cloud services and software licenses under one agreement. They are designed for medium or large organizations that want to license Microsoft products and services organization-wide over a three-year period, with options to purchase perpetual licenses with SA or subscribe to cloud services.
qa_pairs.evidence: Enterprise Agreements offer large organizations a manageable volume licensing program that gives them the flexibility to buy cloud services and software licenses under one agreement. Enterprise Agreements are designed for medium or large organizations that want to license Microsoft products and services organization-wide over a three-year period. Organizations can elect to purchase perpetual licenses (covered with SA) and/or subscribe to cloud services.

qa_pairs.question: What is Microsoft's total revenue for fiscal year 2024 and how does it compare to fiscal year 2023?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's total revenue for fiscal year 2024 was $245.1 billion, compared to $211.9 billion in fiscal year 2023, representing a 16% increase. The growth was driven by increases across each of the company's three segments.
qa_pairs.evidence:
| Revenue |  | $ | 245,122 |  |  | $ | 211,915 |  |  |  | 16% |  |
Revenue increased $33.2 billion or 16% driven by growth across each of our segments. Intelligent Cloud revenue increased driven by Azure. Productivity and Business Processes revenue increased driven by Office 365 Commercial. More Personal Computing revenue increased driven by Gaming.

qa_pairs.question: What was Microsoft Cloud revenue in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 23% to $137.4 billion in fiscal year 2024. Microsoft Cloud revenue comprises revenue from Azure and other cloud services, Office 365 Commercial, the commercial portion of LinkedIn, Dynamics 365, and other commercial cloud properties.
qa_pairs.evidence: Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 23% to $137.4 billion.

qa_pairs.question: What was Microsoft's net income for fiscal year 2024 compared to fiscal year 2023?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's net income for fiscal year 2024 was $88.1 billion, compared to $72.4 billion in fiscal year 2023, representing a 22% increase. Diluted earnings per share also increased 22% from $9.68 to $11.80.

qa_pairs.question: What was Microsoft's operating income for fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's operating income for fiscal year 2024 was $109.4 billion, an increase of $20.9 billion or 24% compared to $88.5 billion in fiscal year 2023. The growth was driven by increases across each of the company's three operating segments.
qa_pairs.evidence:
| Operating income |  |  | 109,433 |  |  |  | 88,523 |  |  |  | 24% |  |
Operating income increased $20.9 billion or 24% driven by growth across each of our segments.

qa_pairs.question: How did the Productivity and Business Processes segment perform in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The Productivity and Business Processes segment generated revenue of $77.7 billion in fiscal year 2024, a 12% increase from $69.3 billion. Operating income grew 19% to $40.5 billion. The growth was driven by Office 365 Commercial revenue growth of 16%, LinkedIn revenue growth of 9%, and Dynamics 365 revenue growth of 24%.
qa_pairs.evidence:
| Productivity and Business Processes |  | $ | 77,728 |  |  | $ | 69,274 |  |  |  | 12% |  |
Productivity and Business Processes
Revenue increased $8.5 billion or 12%.
•
Office Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased $5.8 billion or 14%. Office 365 Commercial revenue grew 16% with seat growth of 7%, driven by small and medium business and frontline worker offerings, as well as growth in revenue per user. Office Commercial products revenue declined 16% driven by continued customer shift to cloud offerings.

qa_pairs.question: How did the More Personal Computing segment perform in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The More Personal Computing segment generated revenue of $62.0 billion in fiscal year 2024, a 13% increase from $54.7 billion in fiscal year 2023. Operating income grew 17% to $19.3 billion. Growth was driven primarily by Gaming revenue, which increased 39%, largely due to the Activision Blizzard acquisition.
qa_pairs.evidence:
| More Personal Computing |  |  | 62,032 |  |  |  | 54,734 |  |  |  | 13% |  |
Gaming revenue increased $6.0 billion or 39% driven by growth in Xbox content and services. Xbox content and services revenue increased 50% driven by 44 points of net impact from the Activision Blizzard acquisition. Xbox hardware revenue decreased 13% driven by lower volume of consoles sold.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to Devices revenue in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Devices revenue decreased 15%, or $815 million, in fiscal year 2024 compared to fiscal year 2023. Despite this decline, the More Personal Computing segment's gross margin percentage increased, driven by sales mix shift to higher margin businesses and improvement in Devices margins.
qa_pairs.evidence: Devices revenue decreased $815 million or 15%.

qa_pairs.question: How did Xbox content and services revenue change in fiscal year 2024 and what drove the change?
qa_pairs.answer: Xbox content and services revenue increased 50% in fiscal year 2024, driven by 44 points of net impact from the Activision Blizzard acquisition. The net impact reflects the change of Activision Blizzard content from third-party to first-party. However, Xbox hardware revenue decreased 13% due to lower volume of consoles sold.
qa_pairs.evidence: Xbox content and services revenue increased 50% driven by 44 points of net impact from the Activision Blizzard Inc. (“Activision Blizzard”) acquisition. The net impact reflects the change of Activision Blizzard content from third-party to first-party.

qa_pairs.question: What was Microsoft's research and development expense in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's research and development expenses were $29.5 billion in fiscal year 2024, an increase of $2.3 billion or 9% compared to $27.2 billion in fiscal year 2023. The increase was driven by Gaming, with 7 points of growth from the Activision Blizzard acquisition, and investments in cloud engineering.
qa_pairs.evidence: Research and development expenses increased $2.3 billion or 9% driven by Gaming, with 7 points of growth from the Activision Blizzard acquisition, and investments in cloud engineering.

qa_pairs.question: Why was Microsoft's effective tax rate lower than the U.S. federal statutory rate?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's effective tax rate was lower than the U.S. federal statutory rate primarily due to earnings taxed at lower rates in foreign jurisdictions resulting from producing and distributing products and services through the company's foreign regional operations center in Ireland.
qa_pairs.evidence: Our effective tax rate was lower than the U.S. federal statutory rate, primarily due to earnings taxed at lower rates in foreign jurisdictions resulting from producing and distributing our products and services through our foreign regional operations center in Ireland.

qa_pairs.question: What was Microsoft's cash flow from operations in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Cash from operations increased $31.0 billion to $118.5 billion for fiscal year 2024, primarily due to an increase in cash received from customers. This represents a significant increase from $87.6 billion in fiscal year 2023.
qa_pairs.evidence: Cash from operations increased $31.0 billion to $118.5 billion for fiscal year 2024, primarily due to an increase in cash received from customers.

qa_pairs.question: How much did Microsoft spend on share repurchases and dividends in fiscal year 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: During fiscal year 2024, Microsoft repurchased 32 million shares for $12.0 billion and declared dividends totaling $22.3 billion. In comparison, in fiscal year 2023, the company repurchased 69 million shares for $18.4 billion and declared dividends of $20.2 billion.
qa_pairs.evidence:
During fiscal years 2024 and 2023, we repurchased 32 million shares and 69 million shares of our common stock for $12.0 billion and $18.4 billion, respectively, through our share repurchase program. All repurchases were made using cash resources. As of June 30, 2024, $10.3 billion remained of our $60 billion share repurchase program. Refer to Note 16 – Stockholders’ Equity of the Notes to Financial Statements (Part II, Item 8 of this Form 10-K) for further discussion.
Dividends
During fiscal years 2024 and 2023, our Board of Directors declared dividends totaling $22.3 billion and $20.2 billion, respectively. We intend to continue returning capital to shareholders in the form of dividends, subject to declaration by our Board of Directors. Refer to Note 16 – Stockholders’ Equity of the Notes to Financial Statements (Part II, Item 8 of this Form 10-K) for further discussion.

qa_pairs.question: What was the remaining authorization under Microsoft's share repurchase program as of June 30, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: As of June 30, 2024, $10.3 billion remained of Microsoft's $60 billion share repurchase program. All repurchases were made using cash resources and may occur through open market purchases or pursuant to a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan.
qa_pairs.evidence: As of June 30, 2024, $10.3 billion remained of our $60 billion share repurchase program.

qa_pairs.question: What were Microsoft's total assets as of June 30, 2024, compared to June 30, 2023?
qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's total assets as of June 30, 2024 were $512.2 billion, up significantly from $412.0 billion as of June 30, 2023. The increase was driven largely by growth in goodwill (from $67.9 billion to $119.2 billion due to the Activision Blizzard acquisition), property and equipment (from $95.6 billion to $135.6 billion), and intangible assets (from $9.4 billion to $27.6 billion).

qa_pairs.answer: Microsoft's total stockholders' equity as of June 30, 2024 was $268.5 billion, up from $206.2 billion as of June 30, 2023. This consisted of common stock and paid-in capital of $100.9 billion, retained earnings of $173.1 billion, and accumulated other comprehensive loss of $5.6 billion.
